name: Ding Gu (Zijian)
page_title: Ding Gu (Zijian) 丁固 (子賤) [Wu] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives
ranks: Minister of Justice, Left Imperial Counselor, Minister over the Masses
historical_notes:
Originally named Ding Mi. After Sun Hao came to power, Ding Mi changed his name to Ding Gu in order to avoid the taboo on the name of Sun Hao’s father-in-law Teng Mi. Out of respect for Ding Mi, Teng Mi changed his name to Teng Mu.

Ding Gu held the rank of Minister of Justice under Sun Xiu. When Sun Hao became emperor in 264, he made Ding Gu Left Imperial Counselor (Zuǒ Yùshĭ Dàfū 左御史大夫). In 268, Sun Hao made Ding Gu Minister over the Masses (Sītú 司徒).

sections.short-biography: An Excellency, he had a dream that prophesied about Sun Hao.

full_text:
Ding Gu (Zijian) 丁固 (子賤)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Ding Gu (丁固), styledZijian (子賤), was born in from Shanyin. An Excellency, he had a dream that prophesied about Sun Hao. Ding Gu was affiliated with the Wu Kingdom.

BiographyHistoric (Confirmed)
Originally named Ding Mi. After Sun Hao came to power, Ding Mi changed his name to Ding Gu in order to avoid the taboo on the name of Sun Hao’s father-in-law Teng Mi. Out of respect for Ding Mi, Teng Mi changed his name to Teng Mu.
Ding Gu held the rank of Minister of Justice under Sun Xiu. When Sun Hao became emperor in 264, he made Ding Gu Left Imperial Counselor (Zuǒ Yùshĭ Dàfū 左御史大夫). In 268, Sun Hao made Ding Gu Minister over the Masses (Sītú 司徒).
